premises.content: Conventional (phonon-mediated, BCS-type) superconductivity has been demonstrated above 190 K (arXiv) / 203 K (Nature) — the highest Tc for any superconductor at the time of publication. This breaks the long-standing preconception that BCS superconductors are limited to Tc ~ 30 K and surpasses even the cuprate record of 164 K under pressure.
premises.label: conventional_sc_above_200k

premises.content: Deuterium substitution reveals a strong isotope effect: the Fm-3m phase (LaH₁₀) has Tc = 250 K (H) → 168 K (D), giving isotope exponent α ≈ 0.57. The competing P4/nmm phase (LaH₁₁) shows Tc = 215 K (H) → 140 K (D), giving α ≈ 0.62. Both values are close to the BCS harmonic limit α = 0.5, with the slight excess attributable to soft anharmonicity of H/D vibrations (ωH/ωD < √2).
premises.label: isotope_effect

premises.content: La (99.9%, Alfa Aesar) was loaded with H₂ (99.999%) in a DAC. La readily reacts with H₂ to form LaH₃ at ~10 GPa without heating. After pressurizing to 120-190 GPa, pulsed YAG laser heating (spot ~10 μm, T < 700-1500 K) was used to initiate the formation of higher hydrides. The laser spot was scanned over the sample for uniform reaction.
premises.label: synthesis_method

premises.content: At zero field, the R(T) transition shows two steps near 245 K and 230 K. The higher-temperature step gradually broadens with increasing magnetic field and completely disappears above 3 T. This behavior is consistent with inhomogeneous superconductivity — multiple superconducting regions with slightly different Tc values, as seen in disordered boron-doped diamond.
premises.label: two_step_transition

premises.content: X-ray diffraction at 150 GPa identifies the Tc = 250 K phase as face-centered cubic Fm-3m with refined lattice constant a = 5.1019(5) Å (V = 132.80 Å³). The volume matches DFT predictions for LaH₁₀ (131.9 Å³ at 172 GPa). Stoichiometry LaH₉.₆ estimated from the hydrogen volume (VH = 1.9 Å³/atom from LaH₃ calibration) — in good agreement with LaH₁₀.
premises.label: xrd_fm3m_structure
